mnemonics: W.I.T.T. - What It Takes To Time: Wall, Ideal, Total, Throughput
keyPoints: Use wall-clock time (total elapsed time) to measure the performance of a parallel program.
Measure the ideal time by running the best possible sequential algorithm on one processor.
The total CPU time (sum of time used by all processors) will always be greater than the wall-clock time due to parallel overhead.
commonMistakes: Using CPU time instead of wall-clock time to measure parallel performance.
Forgetting to account for parallel overhead (communication, synchronization) when comparing to ideal time.
Timing sections of code that include unnecessary initialization or output operations.
